sources: Google Gemini Structured Output docs — 'Include your response schema as part of the responseSchema field only. Don't duplicate the schema in your input prompt, as the generated output might be lower in quality.'
Google Developers Blog — Mastering Controlled Generation with Gemini 1.5: Schema Adherence for Developers

Medium — Structured Output for Beginners: 3 Must-Know Prompting Tips: 'By forcing the model to fill out a reasoning field first, it performs computation in the token space before committing to the final answer'
tokenoptimize.dev / redis.io — 'Avoid restating the same constraint in multiple places, which dilutes instruction attention'; repeated rules waste tokens with no reliability benefit
validations.note: Score 3.5/5 is well-supported. The evaluator's specific claims are all verifiable: (1) SYSTEM_PROMPT duplication across systemInstruction and packet body is a confirmed anti-pattern per Google's own docs; (2) reasoning-before-commitment ({reason, score} field ordering) is a validated CoT technique; (3) competitor-rule restatement 4x and EVALUATE-not-EXPAND restatement 3x are confirmed token-wasting consolidation debts per token-efficiency literature. The deduction from 5 is appropriate given the documented consolidation backlog.

sources: Google Gemini Structured Output docs (ai.google.dev) — responseSchema + responseMimeType='application/json' for constrained decoding; 'Constrained decoding is the only method that will guarantee the output adheres to the schema'

Google Gemini Structured Output docs — 'For reasoning tasks, JSON-Prompt is recommended over JSON-Schema' — supports the design decision that Builder 1 (google_search + reasoning) intentionally omits responseSchema
genaiunplugged.substack.com / Google Cloud docs — temperature 0.0–0.3 is industry consensus for structured JSON on Gemini 2.5 Pro; the codebase's temperature=0.1 is within the recommended band
ofox.ai 2026 AI Error Handling guide — model-tier split (flash for simpler tasks, pro for reasoning) is a documented cost-optimization pattern
validations.note: Score 4.25/5 is well-supported. The three positive claims (temperature=0.1 correct, responseSchema on builders 2+3 correct, builder 1 omitting responseSchema for google_search compatibility correct) all align with authoritative Gemini docs. The remaining deduction is justified: (1) gemini-2.5-pro hardcoded for enrich_persona when flash suffices is a confirmed cost anti-pattern; (2) builder 1's structural inability to carry responseSchema is a documented Gemini limitation (tool + responseSchema incompatibility). Score is accurate.

sources: fast.io — AI Agent Retry Patterns 2026: '3 retries max, base delay 1-2s, exponential factor 2x, add full or equal jitter' — matches the codebase's 3 attempts + full jitter capped at 30s
fast.io — 'Respect Retry-After header on 429 responses' — evaluator correctly notes Retry-After numeric-seconds form is parsed; HTTP-date form gap is a confirmed RFC 7231 compliance gap

apxml.com — 'Distinguish API failure retries (retry same prompt) from validation failure retries (retry with modified prompt that includes the error)' — evaluator's observation about builder rotation coupling is directly supported

Medium — Error Handling & Retries: Making LLM Calls Reliable (Feb 2026): 4xx except 429 → non-retryable; safety blocks → permanent; 5xx + 429 → retryable with backoff — matches the three-class hierarchy (GeminiPermanentError / GeminiRateLimitError / GeminiTransientError)
ofox.ai 2026 — per-field validation error injection into retry context is the recommended validation-and-repair pattern
validations.note: Score 4.8/5 is well-supported. The per-field structural floor (validate_review_output with precise field+index error strings) injected into retry context is confirmed best practice per apxml.com. The two minor remaining gaps — HTTP-date Retry-After form and builder-rotation coupling to attempt index — are real and documented by RFC 7231 and apxml.com respectively. They are correctly rated as polish items that don't materially affect production reliability, justifying the 4.8 rather than 5.0.

sources: python.useinstructor.com — Advanced Caching Strategies for Python LLM Applications: lru_cache as L1 in-memory cache is a validated pattern; 'cache hits are 207,636x faster than misses'
tokenoptimize.dev — Cache-Aside pattern: 'Cache at every safe layer: exact-match for identical inputs; for cross-run persistence use a file-backed store keyed by stable content hash'
redis.io LLM Token Optimization 2026 — 'Avoid duplicate upstream work (fetching the same webpage twice per request is a pure waste). Cache by a deterministic key.'
tokenoptimize.dev — persona caching by (service, type, description) hash is a standard pattern for eliminating redundant LLM calls in batch evaluation workloads
validations.note: Score 3.75/5 is supported. The lru_cache fix (duplicate-crawl elimination) is a validated improvement. The remaining gaps — no persona cache by content hash, 3 divergent builder shapes, no model-tier split — are confirmed sub-optimal patterns per caching and pipeline structure literature. The score accurately reflects partial progress (lru_cache done) with notable remaining topology debt.

sources: redis.io LLM Token Optimization 2026 — 'Placing stable constraints in systemInstruction enables prompt caching on supported endpoints'; duplicate system prompt across systemInstruction and user message doubles token cost for those sections

dasroot.net Token Optimization LLM Costs 2026 — 'Repeating lengthy system prompts across every query compounds the problem'; competitive-rule restatement 4x and EVALUATE-not-EXPAND 3x are confirmed waste patterns

daydreamsoft.com Context Window Optimization 2026 — 'Relevance filtering: rank context based on relevance, maximize signal and minimize noise'; unfiltered 4.4k token webpage excerpt is a confirmed optimization target

oneuptime.com Context Compression 2026 — 'Context compression by removing redundant, irrelevant, or low-value content before it reaches the model; structured context management can reduce 140k to 6k tokens' — supports the evaluator's relevance-filter recommendation for webpage excerpts
Google Gemini docs — 'Include schema in responseSchema only, not in prompt' — the iter-3 schema-removal win (500–800 tokens saved) is confirmed by Google's own guidance
validations.note: Score 3.5/5 is well-supported. The evaluator's token breakdown is verifiable: SYSTEM_PROMPT duplication (~600 tokens), competitor-rule restatement x4 (~300 tokens), EVALUATE-not-EXPAND x3 (~200 tokens), unfiltered webpage excerpt (~4.4k tokens) are all confirmed waste patterns per 2026 token-efficiency literature. The iter-3 schema removal (~500-800 tokens saved) is confirmed best practice per Google Gemini docs. The scoring rubric retention as a quality-positive tradeoff is a defensible judgment call.
